description_long: This apartment building features a steeply pitched, shingled roof with two dormer windows and a prominent stone chimney. The facade is composed of a lower section with stonework and an upper section finished in a muted beige plaster, punctuated by symmetrically arranged windows with white shutters. The main entrance is a large, arched green door set into the stone base, flanked by shuttered windows. The side and rear elevations are largely unadorned, with plain plastered walls and minimal fenestration, emphasizing the building's verticality and solid massing. The overall appearance suggests a historic European urban context, possibly French, with classic proportions and restrained decorative elements. The structure appears robust and built primarily from masonry, with a weathered, timeworn finish.
description: A historic, masonry apartment building with a steep shingled roof, dormer windows, and a large arched entrance. The facade features symmetrical windows and a prominent stone chimney, evoking classic European urban architecture.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main arched green entrance, two dormer windows on the steep roof, and symmetrical facade windows with shutters.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by a plain, plastered wall and the prominent stone chimney, with minimal window openings.
description_view_2: Angled view highlighting the transition from the stone base to the plastered upper facade, with the arched entrance and partial side wall visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a large, unadorned plastered wall with no windows or doors, emphasizing the building's solid mass.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are symmetrically arranged on the front facade, with two dormer windows on the roof and evenly spaced windows with shutters on the main level. The side and rear walls have minimal or no windows, emphasizing a formal, balanced front.
